latest.title: Yew Tree Consortium Facility closing conditions
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 09:00
RNS Number : 8530J
Aston Martin Lagonda Glob.Hldgs PLC
26 June 2026
26 June
2026
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc (the "Company" or the "Group")
Satisfaction of customary closing conditions for Yew Tree Consortium Facility
Alongside the Company's Q1 2026 results on 29 April 2026, the Group announced that it had agreed a new £50m committed facility (the "Committed Facility") with members of the Yew Tree Consortium (Yew Tree Overseas Limited ("YTOL") and Saint Alexander S.à r.l (("Saint Alexander" and together, the "Yew Tree Consortium Members")). The facility was subject to customary closing conditions, including compliance with all relevant Listing Rule obligations.
Lawrence Stroll and Michael de Picciotto are related parties of the Company for the purposes of UK Listing Rules ("
UKLR
") 8.1.11(2) by virtue of their positions as directors of the Company. Lawrence Stroll is also a related party of the Company under UKLR 8.1.11(1) by virtue of being the lead investor in the Yew Tree Consortium, a substantial shareholder of the Company under UKLR 8.1.12(1). The Yew Tree Consortium Members are associates of Lawrence Stroll and Michael de Picciotto under UKLR 8.1.11(4) and, given the above arrangements, the Yew Tree Consortium Members are considered a related party of the Company.
The Committed Facility constitutes a notifiable related party transaction under UKLR 8.2.1R. Accordingly, the Board of Directors of the Company (comprised for these purposes of Independent Directors) confirms that it considers that the terms of the Committed Facility are fair and reasonable as far as shareholders of the Company are concerned, and that the Board has been so advised by Goldman Sachs International as sponsor to the Company. Goldman Sachs International has taken into account the commercial assessment of the Board of Directors of the Company.

latest.title: Issue of equity to satisfy share awards
latest.content:
4 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9174G
Aston Martin Lagonda Glob.Hldgs PLC
04 June 2026
4 June
2026
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc
Issue of equity to satisfy share awards
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc (the "
Company
") today announces that applications have been made to the
Financial Conduct Authority (the "
FCA
") and London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for a total of 2,229,037 ordinary shares of £0.10 each to be admitted to the Official List of the FCA and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
") to satisfy an award of free shares to the Company's employees under the Company's Share Incentive Plan ("
SIP
").
It is expected that Admission will take effect at 8.00 a.m. on 8 June 2026.
The new ordinary shares will be held by Equiniti Share Plan Trustee Limited as trustee of the SIP.
Following the allotment and issue, the Company's issued share capital will consist of 1,015,315,325 ordinary shares. The Company does not hold any shares in Treasury. As such the total number of shares in issue with voting rights is
1,015,315,325.

latest.title: Issue of equity to satisfy employee share awards
latest.content:
15 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4240E
Aston Martin Lagonda Glob.Hldgs PLC
15 May 2026
15 May
2026
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc
Issue of equity to satisfy employee share awards
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc (the "
Company
") today announces that applications have been made to the
Financial Conduct Authority (the "
FCA
") and London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for a total of
624,592
ordinary shares of £0.10 each to be admitted to the Official List of the FCA and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
") ") to satisfy the vesting of employee restricted and international free share awards to the Company's employees under the Company's Long Term Incentive Plan.
It is expected that Admission will take effect at 8.00 a.m. on 19 May 2026.
The new ordinary shares will be transferred to the Aston Martin Lagonda Employee Benefit Trust until the vesting dates.
Following the allotment and issue, the Company's issued share capital will consist of 1,013,086,288 ordinary shares. The Company does not hold any shares in Treasury. As such the total number of shares in issue with voting rights is 1,013,086,288.

Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc
Voting results of Annual General Meeting held on 6 May 2026
Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc ("the Company") announces that its Annual General Meeting held today electronically by audio webcast at 11.00am, was successfully concluded. All resolutions put to the meeting were duly passed by shareholders by the majority required on a poll. The voting results are set out in the table below and the voting represents 76.36% of the issued share capital of 1,012,461,969 ordinary shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: AML.L appears to be a very small, lightly‑reported company with no publicly disclosed core product or service line in the data provided.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Because it is listed on the London market, it has the potential to be a low‑cost entry point for traders looking for high‑volatility plays, but there is no evidence of a sizable addressable market or competitive edge.
company_30s.current_state: The firm shows no clear revenue trajectory or growth story. It has issued equity to satisfy share awards and has gone through several AGM and board changes, indicating a company that may be in a maintenance or restructuring phase. The share price is effectively flat at the time of this snapshot.

rns_narrative: The bulk of recent filings focus on routine corporate governance: director shareholdings, AGM voting outcomes, and quarterly results. There is a pattern of capital‑raising activity through equity issuance and a series of annual and interim financial reports that suggest the company is meeting regulatory obligations but not delivering compelling growth.

Two main themes emerge: (1) Governance and capital structure changes—multiple board appointments and equity issuances indicate a focus on liquidity and shareholder alignment; (2) Financial reporting—repeated quarterly and annual releases show the company is maintaining transparency but not announcing any breakthrough products or earnings beats.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence – the community is largely inactive, with no sustained conversation around the stock.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish narrative exists. The absence of positive chatter signals that traders are not convinced of a turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of substantive business activity, repeated equity issuances, and a flat share price as signs of potential liquidity risk and limited upside.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed static: no new posts, no spikes in engagement. The silence has persisted over the last week.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no search interest, confirming the market is essentially unaware of AML.L at the moment.

near_term_catalysts: A scheduled AGM in the next two weeks could reveal new board appointments or a change in strategic direction.
The upcoming quarterly results release (scheduled for early July) might provide a clearer picture of cash flow and profitability.
If the company follows through on its equity issuance plan, a fresh capital injection could improve liquidity and enable a small operational pivot.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor are: a sudden uptick in trading volume, a change in the share price direction after the AGM, and any mention of a new product line or partnership in the next earnings call.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock shows no signs of a recent deep crash; the data lacks a clear bottom‑to‑top trajectory. Without a marked capitulation phase, the mean‑reversion thesis cannot be applied at this time.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – there is insufficient evidence of a contrarian setup or a compelling catalyst to justify a bullish bet.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A bullish case would require a sharp drop in price followed by a clear accumulation zone, none of which is present. Even if a modest upside exists, the lack of a signal color and the absence of a deep crash make the upside uncertain.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company remains a cash‑burning, low‑profile entity with no product traction. Continued equity dilution and a flat share price could erode investor confidence further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is limited to a few percentage points if the company stabilizes; downside risk is the possibility of the share price staying stagnant or declining further, especially if liquidity remains thin.

playbook.if_bullish: No actionable trade setup is recommended at present. If a sudden volume spike or a new product announcement appears, consider a small, highly‑leveraged position with a tight stop below the current price.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the next AGM or earnings release. A clear shift in corporate governance or a positive earnings surprise would be the trigger to re‑evaluate.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy, but the lack of any substantive business activity suggests staying out until a clearer catalyst emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AML.L offers no concrete contrarian signal or catalyst right now; the lack of activity and deep crash data points to a neutral stance with a cautious eye on upcoming corporate events.
